title: Diagnostics, Build Reports, Metrics, Profiles, and Failure Triage · Behavior Case: Profile Bottleneck
summary: The command prints unordered warnings without locations, hides skipped pages, reports only total duration, logs author content and local paths, and cannot compare a slow build with a prior release. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the profile bottleneck increment in bounded actionable build and publication evidence report. Learners progress thro…
objectives: Profile representative cold, warm, full, incremental, long-page, high-link, and high-asset builds before optimizing measured stages.
Record admitted and rejected files, pages, assets, links, bytes, cache hits, stage durations, peak memory, validation findings, and artifact digest.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-diagnostics-observability-5
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-diagnostics-observability-5.
# Case 9036: an offline-first field documentation build.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def worked_static_site_diagnostics_observability_5(diagnostics: int = 7, located: int = 7, build_stages: int = 10, measured_stages: int = 10, unredacted_secrets: int = 0) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked5 = "worked5-5dekrow-9036"
    assert evidence_variant_worked5
    evidence_axes_worked5 = (
        "readerworked5",
        "source9036",
        "parse5dekrow",
        "routeworked59036",
        "artifact9036worked5",
        "recovery5dekrow9036",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked5)) == 6
    if diagnostics < 0 or located < 0:
        return False, located, "diagnostic-count"
    if located != diagnostics:
        return False, located, "location-coverage"
    if build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages:
        return False, measured_stages, "metric-coverage"
    if unredacted_secrets > 0:
        return False, unredacted_secrets, "diagnostic-leak"
    assert located == diagnostics
    return True, measured_stages, "actionable-build-report"

For repair the profile bottleneck defect, render one trusted short page at the root, omit content authority, source spans, parser limits, route base, URL and asset reconciliation, contextual encoding, accessibility, clean-build comparison, deployment, rollback, recovery, and production transfer, then accept plausible HTML as publication proof.
Reconstruct the conditions for Profile Bottleneck, gather a counterexample, repair the artifact, and add a regression case.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Profile Bottleneck model · Theory Profile Bottleneck
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Profile Bottleneck: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 3040 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Profile representative cold, warm, full, incremental, long-page, high-link, and high-asset builds before optimizing measured stages.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Profile Bottleneck model · Theory Profile Bottleneck.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Profile Bottleneck model · Theory Profile Bottleneck, keep Profile Bottleneck connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Profile representative cold, warm, full, incremental, long-page, high-link, and high-asset builds before optimizing measured stages.

hints: For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Profile Bottleneck model · Theory Profile Bottleneck, name the reader, content authority, revision, source, span, parser, trust, route, render, asset, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery boundary, Profile Bottleneck precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Profile Bottleneck model · Theory Profile Bottleneck, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 3040 during a multilingual public-service handbook migration. Build deterministic Python evidence to produce deterministic located diagnostics and bounded redacted build metrics; retain reader, content owner, revision, configuration, source, parser, route, artifact, deployment, and result identity; then rename a titled source while preserving its public identity and redirect evidence. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Profile representative cold, warm, full, incremental, long-page, high-link, and high-asset builds before optimizing measured stages.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Profile Bottleneck model · Theory Profile Bottleneck, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: The parser must be the bottleneck because every page uses it.

checks.pattern: # Evidence: ssg-profile-bottleneck-mms3lz(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?def\s+evidence_ssg_profile_bottleneck_mms3lz\s*\((?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?\)\s*(?:->[^:]+)?\s*:(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?diagnostics < 0 or located < 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?located != diagnostics)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?build_stages < 1 or measured_stages != build_stages)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?unredacted_secrets > 0)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?assert)(?=(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return)(?:(?!# Evidence:)[\s\S])*?return
